interpretation: Q4_K_M deterministic IDs are restored to the previous correctness-good baseline: 512/128 -> 318 and 4K/128 -> 220.
Tracked peak remains 22.487 GiB and sampled HIP-used remains about 23.04 GiB, so the restored path still fits the 24 GiB-class GPU1 short gates.

This is a correctness recovery, not a new performance promotion claim; future optimization should start from this corrected Q4_K_M baseline, and any raw-Q8 or dense-Q4 raw memory tradeoff must be split and explicitly reviewed.
After correctness recovery, hipEngine Q4_K_M pp512 still trails user llama.cpp HIP by 18.61% and Vulkan by 3.55%; decode remains ahead of both llama.cpp tg128 rows.
